channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Quebec Innu community rejects $2.5 billion hydro deal in referendum
item.description: Members of the Pessamit Innu First Nation, located northeast of Quebec City, have rejected a proposed $2.5 billion hydro development agreement with Hydro-Québec and the provincial government. The refe...

item.description: The provided text aggregates various global news topics including sports (Kospi index, cricket players like Katie Boulter and Alex De Minaur), financial data (gold rate, stock market trends), geopolit...

item.title: Migori MCA Urges President William Ruto to Extend Presidency Beyond Term Limits
item.description: A Migori County Assembly member, George Omamba, has publicly called for President William Ruto to extend his term beyond the constitutional two-term limit. During a meeting at State House Nairobi with...

item.title: Somali Migrants Choose to Remain in South Africa Amid Anti-Immigrant Protests
item.description: The article highlights the decision of Somali migrants to stay in South Africa despite growing anti-immigrant protests and government crackdowns. Amir Sheikh, chairperson of the African Diaspora Forum...

item.title: Kia Tasman: A refined pickup redefining segment standards
item.description: The Kia Tasman emerges as a sophisticated double cab, blending aggressive design with premium refinement. This new Korean contender challenges traditional pickup norms, offering a cabin experience aki...

item.title: ERC Clarifies Consumers Don't Need to Pay Full Disputed Electricity Bills
item.description: The Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC) has clarified that consumers disputing electricity bills do not need to pay the full contested amount immediately, addressing concerns about unfair billing pract...

item.title: Sokoto teachers' pay dispute sparks parent-teacher collaboration demands
item.description: Parents of students at Federal Government College (FGC) Staff School in Sokoto have raised concerns over the current N60,000 monthly salary for teachers, calling it insufficient for educators with ove...

item.title: Former Tory Minister Ann Widdecombe Found Dead in Murder Case
item.description: Ann Widdecombe, a former Conservative minister and prominent political figure, was found dead at her home in Dartmoor, Devon, following a sudden disappearance. Police confirmed her death as murder, wi...

item.title: Cadaver Fat Injectable Gains Attention as GLP-1 Weight Loss Drives Demand for Body Contouring
item.description: A new injectable body contouring product called alloClae is attracting growing attention in the U.S. because it is made from donated human fat obtained from cadaver tissue. Developed by Tiger Aestheti...

item.title: Top Cartoons from July 14, 2026: A Collection of Political Commentary
item.description: The article features a curated selection of political cartoons published on July 14, 2026, highlighting key issues in Australian politics. The cartoons critique government policies, electoral processe...

item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook, has announced significant expansion plans for its AI data center operations in Louisiana. The move is part of the company's broader strategy to enhance its global...

item.title: UK Police Investigate Suspected Murder of Former MP Ann Widdecombe
item.description: Former British lawmaker Ann Widdecombe, 78, was found dead in her home in Devon, England, on July 13, 2026. British counter-terrorism police are now leading the investigation into her suspected murder...

item.title: Thika High School Student Dies in Prayer-Related Fight
item.description: A Grade 10 student died at Thika High School on July 11, sparking nationwide grief in Kenya. The principal, Ndungu Nyoro, shared details of the incident during an interview with Kiambu News, describin...

item.title: Dubai and Tanzania Strengthen Tourism Collaboration for Mutual Growth
item.description: Dubai is intensifying efforts to enhance its tourism partnership with Tanzania, aiming to boost visitor numbers through collaboration with local travel industry stakeholders. During a recent roadshow ...

item.title: 56-year-old man faces court after making repeated nuisance calls to emergency services
item.description: A 56-year-old man from Chifley has been arrested and charged with 10 counts of making vexatious calls to emergency services after allegedly harassing operators multiple times. Police confirmed the man...

item.title: Bangkok music bar fire kills 27, injures dozens
item.description: A devastating fire broke out at a music bar in Bangkok, resulting in at least 27 fatalities and over 50 injuries. The incident, which occurred in a crowded establishment, has sparked concerns about fi...

item.title: Philippines Strengthens Legal Efforts to Enforce 2016 South China Sea Ruling
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formed a dedicated maritime unit to sustain the gains from the 2016 South China Sea arbitral ruling, which invalidated China's 'nine-dash line' claim. Justice Secre...

item.title: ADC Denies Fake INEC Portal Upload, Calls for Investigation into Alleged Forgery
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has categorically denied claims that a group uploaded fake names to the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) portal, calling the allegation a delibera...

item.title: David Beckham jokes that Victoria was 'celebrating on the inside' after her muted reaction to England's World Cup goal goes viral
item.description: David Beckham has responded light-heartedly after his wife, Victoria Beckham, became the subject of widespread social media attention during England's World Cup quarter-final victory over Norway in Mi...

item.title: Ebola outbreak in DRC spreads to two new provinces, surpassing 700 deaths
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has expanded to two additional northeastern provinces, Haut-Uele and Tshopo, according to the country's public health institute. The co...
